articles.title: Hip replacement beats exercise-only care for moderate-to-severe hip arthritis in yearlong trial
articles.description: Total hip arthroplasty leads to the best outcomes in the treatment of moderate-to-severe hip osteoarthritis, according to a new study published in The Bone & Joint Journal by the University of Eastern Finland and Kuopio University Hospital. During a one-year follow-up, surgery combined with conservative treatment was found to relieve pain and improve functional capacity and quality of life more effectively than conservative treatment alone, which was based on an exercise program.

Homebridge 2.0 is here, and it speaks Matter
articles.description:
Homebridge, the popular software solution that allows you to integrate non-HomeKit devices like Ring cameras into Apple Home, is adding support for the Matter smart home standard.
Today, May 4th, Homebridge 2.0, which has been in beta for over three years, officially launches. Along with other improvements, the update brings the "initial groundwork for Matter support," according to a post on the r/homebridge subreddit from NorthernMan5, one of the GitHub core maintainers.
The update enables Homebridge to act as a Matter bridge in addition to a HomeKit bridge, allowing Matter devices to appear in Apple Home through Homebridge. It will also  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Proteins found in cancer extracellular vesicles could guide drug development
articles.description: Cancer cells release tiny biological packages called extracellular vesicles (EVs) that help tumors grow and spread. A team led by Cedars-Sinai Health Sciences University investigators has now connected certain proteins carried in these EVs with poor patient outcomes in multiple cancer types. Their findings were published in the Journal of Extracellular Vesicles.

articles.title: Cruise ship at centre of suspected hantavirus outbreak blocked from docking in Cape Verde
articles.description:
Officials say they will not authorise docking ‘to protect public health’ after deaths of three passengers on vessel

What is hantavirus, the infection thought to have killed three on cruise ship?

Officials in Cape Verde have said they will not allow a cruise ship believed to be harbouring an outbreak of a rare respiratory virus to dock in its ports, after the deaths of three passengers onboard.
The statement on Monday came hours after global health officials said they were scrambling to investigate the suspected outbreak of hantavirus, a disease primarily found in rodents, on the cruise ship in the Atlantic.
 Continue reading...

articles.title: X-linked liver enzyme may explain why women and men process cholesterol differently

articles.title: Use of hepatitis C-positive donors reduces pancreas transplant wait times
articles.description: Researchers at Cedars-Sinai Health Sciences University have discovered that utilizing organs from donors who are hepatitis C virus-positive (HCV+) can greatly shorten wait times for those awaiting pancreas transplants. According to their study published in the American Journal of Transplantation, patients receiving HCV+ organs experienced an average reduction of 117 days in wait time, all while maintaining comparable safety and organ function to those receiving organs from HCV-negative donors.

articles.description: UDF’s emphatic win in the Kerala Assembly polls has not only silenced Satheesan’s critics, but reasserted the political supremacy of the Congress leader, who was given the task of reinvigorating the party and its disenchanted workers after he was chosen as the Leader of the Opposition following UDF’s electoral debacle in 2021

articles.title: Kerala Assembly Polls 2026: Shanimol Usman wrests Aroor, P. Prasad strengthens hold on Cherthala
articles.description: Ms. Usman defeats CPI(M)’s sitting MLA Daleema by a margin of 9,324 votes; Mr. Prasad, CPI’s incumbent Minister, wins by a margin of 14,489 votes against K.R. Rajendra Prasad

articles.title: Kyle Busch turns nasty during violent NASCAR race, F-bombs fly in wild radio rant & spectators wreck!
articles.description: Chase Elliott may be back to his dominant form after his Texas win, while Kyle Busch and John Hunter Nemechek left angry after a last-lap incident.

articles.title: Banks Kick Off Sale of Junk Bonds Backing BASF Coatings Deal
articles.description: Banks have kicked off the sale of roughly €1.95 billion-equivalent ($2.28 billion) of junk bonds to help finance BASF SE’s auto paint and finishing division acquisition by Carlyle Group Inc. and partner Qatar Investment Authority.

articles.title: Chiranjeevi, Mahesh Babu, Nani, Vijay Deverakonda react to actor Vijay TVK win in Tamil Nadu poll

topStories.title: 2 missing U.S. soldiers in Morocco were hiking when they fell off cliff, officials say
topStories.description: Aircraft have been searching the Moroccan coastline for two U.S. soldiers who have been missing since Saturday night. According to defense officials, the service members were off-duty and hiking when they fell off a cliff and went crashing into the waves below. Officials say the soldiers were involved in an overseas training mission. Chris Livesay reports.

topStories.description: Pabitra Kar, once a close aide of BJP leader Suvendu Adhikari, is now fielded by the Trinamool Congress against him .

dailyNews.title: Suspected hantavirus outbreak on cruise ship kills 3, health officials say
dailyNews.description: Health officials say six people were sickened and three have died from a suspected hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship. Hantavirus is a rare virus spread by rodents. Ramy Inocencio reports.

dailyNews.title: Yankees radio icon John Sterling dead at 87
dailyNews.description: New York Yankees radio legend John Sterling has died at 87, WFAN announced. Sterling called Yankees games since 1989, delivering iconic home run calls for decades.

dailyNews.title: Trump says U.S. will guide trapped ships through Strait of Hormuz, as gas prices surge
dailyNews.description: President Trump said on Sunday the U.S. "will guide" trapped ships from countries not involved in the war with Iran through the Strait of Hormuz while maintaining the U.S. blockade on Iranian ports. It comes as the key waterway continues to impact gas prices in the U.S. with costs up 35 cents in just the past week.

dailyNews.title: Rudy Giuliani Hospitalized in Critical but Stable Condition
dailyNews.description: Former New York City mayor Rudy Giuliani has been hospitalized in critical but stable condition, according to his spokesperson. The spokesperson did not specify what led to the hospitalization.

dailyNews.title: Trump says U.S. will 'guide' stranded ships through the Strait. And, key Senate races to watch
dailyNews.description: President Trump announced that the U.S will "guide" stranded ships through the Strait of Hormuz. And, Democrats face an uphill battle to gain Senate control. Here are the key races to watch.

dailyNews.title: Hantavirus outbreak suspected in 3 cruise ship deaths
dailyNews.description: Hantavirus outbreak suspected in 3 cruise ship deaths

dailyNews.title: Working-class man visits Europe, experiences immediate disaster
dailyNews.description: David Hookstead traveled to Europe, and experienced some wild moments, including closes calls and a medical disaster on the flight back to the United States.

dailyNews.title: Spanberger won't rule out taxes on gym memberships, streaming services: 'Should be discussed'
dailyNews.description: Gov. Abigail Spanberger, D-Va., leaves door open to taxing services like gym memberships, digital subscriptions and dog grooming in a recent ABC interview.

dailyNews.title: Gen Z turns on classic snacks, forcing major grocery shake-up: 'They don't trust them'
dailyNews.description: Younger consumers are driving snack trends toward simpler ingredients, transparency and smaller pack sizes amid inflation concerns and a hunger for "functional" snacks.

dailyNews.title: Austria expels 3 Russian Embassy staff over suspected antenna spying in Vienna
dailyNews.description: Austria has expelled three Russian Embassy employees suspected of espionage using antennas on diplomatic buildings

dailyNews.title: Novel in-hospital screening method detects cognitive issues
dailyNews.description: More than 40% of older people admitted to U.S. hospitals have dementia, yet only half of them have been diagnosed with memory and cognitive difficulty. Cedars-Sinai Health Sciences University investigators have developed a comprehensive screening method that identifies previously undiagnosed cognitive impairment and dementia in hospitalized patients. Their findings were published in the Journal of the American Geriatrics Society.

dailyNews.title: Here's how medication abortion works with just one drug that's still fully available
dailyNews.description: A federal appeals court ruling restricts telemedicine access to the abortion drug mifepristone for the whole country, at least temporarily. But another drug that remains available is safe and effective at ending early pregnancy.

topStories.title: Trump Says US Navy Will Escort Ships out of Strait of Hormuz
topStories.description: President Donald Trump has announced that the United States military will help guide stranded ships out of the Strait of Hormuz. Iran is responding with new threats, warning that if any foreign armed force approaches or enters the critical passage, it “will be subjected to attack.” NBC’s Gabe Gutierrez reports for TODAY.

topStories.title: Iran live updates: Trump's 'Project Freedom' begins Monday amid attacks in strait
topStories.description: President Donald Trump said he ordered U.S. forces to guide ships and crews from countries that are not involved with the Iran war out of Strait of Hormuz.

topStories.title: Iran threatens ships that take up U.S. offer on Strait of Hormuz transit
topStories.description: Iran says it will attack vessels that take up President Trump's offer for the U.S. military to guide them through the Strait of Hormuz.

topStories.title: Trump administration falls behind on wildfire prevention with risky fire season ahead
topStories.description: Many of the nation's overgrown forests are at high risk of burning. Under the Trump administration, work to reduce flammable vegetation fell by more than a million acres compared to previous years.

topStories.title: Trump Backed Forced Treatment for Homeless People. Utah Shows the Challenges.
topStories.description: State lawmakers declined to back a Trump-inspired plan to move 1,300 homeless people to a campus on the edge of Salt Lake City, but supporters are trying to keep the plan’s spirit alive.

topStories.description: The long-time champion of Trump and former New York mayor is in hospital, according to his spokesman.

dailyNews.title: China Seeks an Advantage With Both Trump and Iran as War Evolves
dailyNews.description: With President Trump’s visit to Beijing looming, China is pushing Iran to negotiate even as its companies export material that could be used by Iran’s military.

dailyNews.description: President Trump announced the U.S. would "guide" ships not involved in the war with Iran out of the Strait of Hormuz. A U.S.-led task force says it has started the operation.

dailyNews.description: Passengers deal with Spirit shutdown fallout; Trump's poll numbers tank as Iran war continues to jack up gas prices.

dailyNews.title: Senator says Trump's actions violates war powers law
dailyNews.description: Democratic Sen. Adam Schiff of California says that while multiple presidents have not gone to Congress for war authorization in the past, President Trump’s actions are a “clear violation” of the War Powers Act of 1973.

dailyNews.title: DOJ chief says U.S. not actively at war with Iran
dailyNews.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche reacts to President Trump saying he doesn't use the word "war" when referring to the U.S. conflict with Iran.

dailyNews.title: Trump Says Colombia Will Accept Deportees, Ending Tariff Standoff
dailyNews.description: The president has made immigration an early priority to start second term, with ICE making more than 900 arrests Sunday.

dailyNews.title: Trump Says He Wants to 'Clean Out' Gaza, Send Refugees to Egypt and Jordan
dailyNews.description: The president, calling the heavily bombed enclave “a demolition site,” proposed relocating Palestinians temporarily or long-term, a move rejected by Arab countries since the war began.
